zh.lifeSummary: 當William de Chesney Lord of Caenby and Glentham於大約1067年出生在英國林可爾史爾凱比時，他的父親Ralph de Chesney24歲，母親Lady Maud de Waterville20歲。他至少有1個女兒。他去世於大約1121年在英國英格蘭林可爾史爾凱比享年56歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当William de Chesney Lord of Caenby and Glentham于大约1067年出生在英国林可爾史爾凱比时，他的父亲Ralph de Chesney24岁，母亲Lady Maud de Waterville20岁。他至少有1个女儿。他去世于大约1121年在英国英格兰林可爾史爾凱比享年56岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Chesney
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and Irish (Antrim, Down): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh, see McChesney .
English (of Norman origin): variant of   Chaney  , a cognate of 3 below.
French: topographic name for someone who lived by or in an oak wood, from Old French chesnai ‘oak grove’ (from chesne ‘oak’). Compare Cheney 2.

variants.name: McChesney
meanings: Scottish (Galloway) and northern Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac (an) tSasanaigh ‘son of the Saxon (i.e. the Englishman)’. The spelling seems to represent Mac Shasanaigh, to be pronounced originally as McHasney, rather than the English spelling ch.

variants.name: Chestnut
meanings: Scottish (Renfrewshire and Lanarkshire) and northern Irish: altered form of Chesney , shortened from McChesney . Its assimilation to the English word chestnut is due to folk etymology, the word itself being first recorded in 1519. The surname was probably taken to Ireland at the time of the 17th-century Plantation of Ulster.
English (Norfolk): probably a variant of Chestney (see Chaney ), altered by folk etymological association with the word chestnut.

variants.name: Chesna
meanings: English and Irish: variant of   Chesney  .

nameMeanings.name: William
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Probably the most successful of all the Old French names of Germanic origin that were introduced to England by the Normans. It is derived from Germanic wil ‘will, desire’ + helm ‘helmet, protection’. The fact that it was borne by the Conqueror himself does not seem to have inhibited its favour with the ‘conquered’ population: in the first century after the Conquest it was the commonest male name of all, and not only among the Normans. In the later Middle Ages it was overtaken by John , but continued to run second to that name until the 20th century, when the picture became more fragmented.

historicEvents.title: Domesday Book
historicEvents.description: Domesday Book is Britain's earliest extant public record with information containing land surveys and landholdings commissioned by William I. The book was completed in 1086.

historicEvents.title: The Crusades
historicEvents.description: A series of religious wars between Christians and Muslims, known as the Crusades, occurred between 1096 and 1291, and played a large role in European Christian dominance in the Middle East.

historicEvents.title: The Battle of Tichebray
historicEvents.description: King Henry I defeated and captured his brother, Robert of Normandy, at the Battle of Tinchebray in 1106. Robert remained in captivity until his death in 1134.

en.lifeSummary: When William de Chesney Lord of Caenby and Glentham was born about 1067, in Caenby, Lincolnshire, England, his father, Ralph de Chesney, was 24 and his mother, Lady Maud de Waterville, was 20. He had at least 1 daughter. He died about 1121, in Caenby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 56.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque William de Chesney Lord of Caenby and Glentham est né environ 1067, à Caenby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, son père, Ralph de Chesney, avait 24 ans et sa mère, Lady Maud de Waterville, avait 20 ans. Il a eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 1121, à Caenby, Lincolnshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 56 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als William de Chesney Lord of Caenby and Glentham um 1067 in Caenby, Lincolnshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Ralph de Chesney 24 und seine Mutter Lady Maud de Waterville 20. Er hatte mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb um 1121 im Alter von 56 Jahren in Caenby, Lincolnshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando William de Chesney Lord of Caenby and Glentham è nato il circa 1067 nel Caenby, Lincolnshire, England, il suo padre, Ralph de Chesney, aveva 24 anni e il suo madre, Lady Maud de Waterville, aveva 20 anni. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlia. Morì circa 1121, nel Caenby, Lincolnshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 56 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: William de Chesney Lord of Caenby and Glentham(이)가 대략 1067년 영국 링코실르 카이비에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Ralph de Chesney(은)는 24세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Maud de Waterville(은)는 20세였다. 그는 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1121년 영국 잉글랜드 링코실르 카이비에서 56세의 나이로 사망했다.

У него была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер около 1121 г. в местности каенби, линколншир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 56 лет.
